research.interface: Enterprise audit log REST API (GET /enterprises/{enterprise}/audit-log, X-GitHub-Api-Version 2026-03-10)

official_sources.title: REST API endpoints for the audit log - GitHub Enterprise Cloud Docs
official_sources.used_for: GET /enterprises/{enterprise}/audit-log; query parameters phrase, include (web default | git | all), after ('A cursor, as given in the Link header. If specified, the query only searches for events after this cursor.'), before, order (desc default | asc), per_page (default 30), page; requires read:audit_log scope; 200 response of event objects with @timestamp, action, actor, actor_id, org, org_id, created_at, _document_id, business fields

official_sources.title: Using the audit log API for your enterprise - GitHub Enterprise Cloud Docs
official_sources.used_for: cursor-based pagination through the Link header's after/before URLs rather than page numbers; 'Timestamps and date fields in the API response are measured in UTC epoch milliseconds'; phrase search syntax joining qualifiers like action: and created:>= with '+'; the 1,750 queries per hour per user+IP rate limit and the guidance to pause before retrying on 403/429

official_sources.title: API versions - GitHub Enterprise Cloud Docs
official_sources.used_for: X-GitHub-Api-Version header; supported versions 2026-03-10 (latest) and 2022-11-28 (default when the header is omitted); unsupported versions receive 410 Gone

research.language_rationale: The SIEM forwarder this exporter feeds is a .NET service, and the enterprise audit log is a documented raw-REST surface (GitHub's official Octokit .NET SDK does not cover the enterprise audit-log endpoint), so HttpClient with System.Text.Json async stream parsing and IAsyncEnumerable is the idiomatic, credible C# integration; xunit on net10.0 hosts the hermetic loopback listener.

verified_facts: The enterprise audit log is read from GET /enterprises/{enterprise}/audit-log with phrase/include/after/before/order/per_page query parameters, a read:audit_log-scoped bearer token, and enterprise-admin rights.

Pagination is cursor-based: the Link response header hands back URLs with opaque after/before cursors and clients follow them rather than computing page numbers; 'after' means only events after that cursor.
Audit-log timestamps (@timestamp, created_at) are UTC epoch milliseconds, and events carry action, actor, org, and _document_id identity fields.
phrase joins search qualifiers such as action: and created:>= with '+', and include selects web (default), git, or all event types while order defaults to desc.
The endpoint budgets 1,750 queries per hour per user+IP; rate-limited integrations should pause before retrying, and REST errors decode as {message, documentation_url}.
Current REST calls send Accept: application/vnd.github+json and X-GitHub-Api-Version: 2026-03-10 (2022-11-28 is only the omitted-header default).
